summary_one_line: A font/buffer that survives Indic GSUB shaping into a syllable with a halant glyph at the matra-search position, reached by broad havoc byte exploration.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that, after harfbuzz's Indic GSUB shaping, yields a non-Malayalam/non-Tamil syllable whose pre-base-matra search (line 1153 loop) lands new_pos on a glyph that passes is_halant() and whose indic_position is not POS_PRE_M (line 1159 true side). This is a deep structural/state property of the shaped glyph buffer, not a literal byte-comparison operand at a fixed offset.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive resolves the true branch in all 10 trials (resolved=10/10, hitcount=35), and its winning seeds carry varied font-table-like ASCII fragments (uu-hant, o-bok, px-) plus long runs of repeated bytes (0x6b/0xeb/0xed/0xd2) produced by havoc expand/copy/insert ops, giving the broad structural diversity needed to produce a halant-bearing Indic syllable. Broad undirected havoc exploration explores the shaping state space without being pulled toward CMP operands.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog blocks the true branch in all 10 trials (blocked=10/10, hitcount=0) and never takes line 1159 true (L row T=0 F=14). Its I2SRandReplace mutator biases mutations toward substituting bytes that match intercepted integer/string CMP operands; at this branch there is NO informative byte-comparison gate (the gate is is_halant on a post-shaping glyph category), so I2S substitutions waste energy and steer the corpus away from the structural diversity that reaches the halant state.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive via plain havoc/token byte mutation (havoc-only). There is no CMP-solvable roadblock here, so cmplog's comparison-substitution mutator confers no advantage and actively diverts mutation energy, making the I2S delta anti-correlated with reaching this state-machine-derived branch.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive>cmplog on the I2S delta, so the I2S technique is anti-correlated with reaching the branch.
naive resolves the branch in all 10 trials while cmplog blocks it in all 10.
cmplog never resolves and stays blocked, with zero hits on the branch.
The blocked side is the true branch of an is_halant test on a glyph reached during pre-base-matra reordering.
new_pos is computed by scanning backward over Indic categories, so the condition depends on post-shaping glyph categories, not a raw input byte comparison.
At the blocker line the winner takes the true side 21 times and the loser takes only the false side 14 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L1159  T=21 F=0  T=0 F=14  if (is_halant (info[new_pos]) &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winning naive seeds contain font-table-like ASCII fragments produced by havoc insert/expand mutators.
Winning naive seeds were produced by havoc byte mutators, not by any CMP-introspection mutator.
cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator that substitutes intercepted CMP operands into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive's mutators are the havoc + token stack with no I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Decisive pair is naive>cmplog (I2S-ANTI): the WINNER is naive, whose mutation stack contains NO I2S, so the winning technique is plain havoc, not I2SRandReplace. Lineage dash-row verification applies only when the winner is a cmplog/vpc I2S find; here the I2S fuzzer is the LOSER, so a winning-seed lineage I2S check is not applicable. The winning naive seeds' mutation_op fields (ByteDecMutator/BytesExpandMutator/BitFlipMutator, BytesCopyMutator, etc.) are all havoc/token operators with no I2S floor row to seek.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating is_halant on a glyph category that is directly set by an exact input-byte comparison (a CMP-solvable roadblock) in which cmplog then resolves the branch faster than naive — that would show the gate is I2S-solvable and the naive advantage is not a havoc-vs-I2S-diversion effect.

weakest_evidence_point: I cannot directly confirm from bytes which shaped glyph becomes the halant at new_pos; the claim that cmplog's I2S diverts energy is inferred from the I2S-anti pair direction plus the non-CMP nature of the is_halant gate, not from seed-level proof of wasted substitutions.
